taskDescription: Click on the key anatomical terms related to the pectoral region and breast.
textField:
*Pectoralis* *major* has three heads: the *clavicular* head from the medial half of the *clavicle*, the *sternocostal* head from the *sternum* and upper costal cartilages, and the *abdominal* head from the rectus sheath. All heads converge to insert on the lateral lip of the *bicipital* *groove* (*intertubercular* *sulcus*) of the *humerus*.

*Pectoralis* *minor* lies deep to pectoralis major, originating from ribs 3-5 and inserting on the *coracoid* process of the *scapula*. The *lateral* *pectoral* nerve supplies the clavicular head, while the *medial* *pectoral* nerve pierces pectoralis minor before reaching the sternocostal head.

The breast receives its arterial supply from the *internal* *mammary* artery and *lateral* *thoracic* artery. Approximately 75% of *lymphatic* drainage goes to the *axillary* lymph nodes. The *pectoral* (*anterior*) group of axillary nodes is the first to be involved in breast cancer spread.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the key anatomical terms of the pectoral region.
Good effort! You identified many key terms.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of the terminology.
